# Executive Summary

**Project Title:** Offline-First R Education Toolkit for Low-Resource Communities (Pilot: Kano, Nigeria)

This project proposes the development of an open-source, offline-first R education toolkit designed for learners in low-resource environments with limited internet access and computing infrastructure. While global demand for data science skills continues to grow, many communities—particularly in regions such as Kano, Nigeria—face significant barriers to accessing structured R programming education due to connectivity challenges, lack of localized content, and limited training capacity.

To address this gap, the project will create a culturally adapted, beginner-friendly curriculum delivered in both English and Hausa, supported by offline-accessible learning materials, including datasets, exercises, and instructional guides. The toolkit will also include a train-the-trainer framework to enable local facilitators to independently deliver R training within their communities.

The project will be piloted in Kano in collaboration with community networks such as R-Ladies Kano, with the goal of training an initial cohort of learners and facilitators. All materials will be released as open-source resources to ensure global accessibility and adaptability.

By focusing on offline accessibility, localization, and scalability, this project aims to strengthen inclusivity within the R ecosystem and provide a replicable model for expanding data science education in underserved communities worldwide.
